heroHeadline: SAF Drone Strike Kills 6 at Zalingei Displacement Camp; Both Sides Escalate Aerial Attacks on Civilian Areas
heroSubtitle: A Sudanese Armed Forces drone struck Al-Hamidiyah IDP camp near Zalingei, Central Darfur at dawn on April 27, killing 6 civilians and injuring 15 others in a camp sheltering thousands of displaced women and children — documented by Emergency Lawyers Sudan and confirmed by AFP. The strike follows RSF drone attacks on El-Obeid over two consecutive days (April 24-25) that killed 20 civilians including 8 children. On Day 1108 of the Sudan Civil War, both sides continue near-daily aerial campaigns against civilian populations across each other's territory, with approximately 700 civilians killed by drone strikes in Q1 2026 alone — a pattern OHCHR and Amnesty International call 'intensifying.' The UN Emergency Relief Coordinator has warned the world is failing Sudan: 34 million people (65% of the population) require urgent humanitarian support, only 16% of the $3 billion 2026 appeal is funded, famine persists in El Fasher and Kadugli, and an estimated 14 million remain displaced in the world's largest displacement crisis.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center, AP, Canada Global Affairs, Middle East Monitor, Chatham House, Emergency Lawyers Sudan, Spacewar/AFP
